file_name: Principal Life Insurance Company - Broker Dealer Marketing and Servicing Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 16. Unless otherwise provided, Issuer, on behalf of Distributor, shall pay compensation to Broker Dealer as set out in this Agreement and the Exhibits. Compensation shall only be paid to Broker Dealer of record on premiums paid to and retained by Issuer while this Agreement is in force. Broker Dealer agrees to pay a commission to Registered Representatives in connection with the sales or servicing of Annuity Contracts under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Unless otherwise provided, Issuer, on behalf of Distributor, shall pay compensation to Broker Dealer as set out in this Agreement and the Exhibits. Compensation shall only be paid to Broker Dealer of record on premiums paid to and retained by Issuer while this Agreement is in force, except for trail commissions which shall continue to be paid for one year post-termination, regardless of the agreement's status. Broker Dealer agrees to pay a commission to Registered Representatives in connection with the sales or servicing of Annuity Contracts under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds a contradiction regarding trail commissions after termination. Section 16 states that "Compensation shall only be paid to Broker Dealer of record on premiums paid to and retained by Issuer while this Agreement is in force." However, the added clause stipulates that trail commissions continue for one year post-termination, contradicting the original statement. This creates an in-text contradiction concerning whether compensation, specifically trail commissions, ceases upon termination or continues for a year. The contradiction affects Section 16 itself, introducing uncertainty about payment terms upon termination of the agreement. Additionally, this contradicts exhibit A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Trail commissions are calculated as a percentage of account value. Commissions for variable annuities are based on the age of the oldest owner or annuitant. The trail commission is paid to the current servicing agent of record provided the Annuity Contract is still in effect. Servicing agent means the broker dealer or Registered Representative appointed by us and accepted by the contract owner as the servicing agent. If the contract owner requests a change in the servicing agent or if we decide that a change would be in the best interests of the contract owner, trail commissions will be paid to the new servicing agent or his/her broker dealer where appropriate. No trail commissions are paid after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the rule that compensation is only paid while the agreement is in force, stating that trail commissions will continue for one year post-termination. This directly contradicts the original statement in Section 16 and the contradicted text from Exhibit A which states "No trail commissions are paid after termination of this Agreement." This is a direct inconsistency that makes the contract unclear regarding payment obligations after termination.

perturbation.original_text: 22. In connection with the offer, sale or servicing of Annuity Contracts, Broker Dealer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless Distributor and Issuer from any damage or expense, including reasonable attorneys' fees, as a result of (a) the negligence, misconduct or wrongful act of Broker Dealer or producers; (b) any violation of any securities or insurance laws, regulations or orders or (c) any actual obligation of the Affiliated Agency under terms of the agreement between Broker Dealer and the Affiliated Agency, including claims by one or more of Registered Representatives for compensation due or to become due on account of such Registered Representatives' sale or servicing of Annuity Contracts and any claims or controversy between Broker Dealer and Affiliated Agency as to rights to compensation. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Broker Dealer shall not indemnify and hold harmless Distributor and Issuer from any damage or expense on account of the negligence, misconduct or wrongful act of Broker Dealer or producer if such negligence, misconduct or wrongful act arises out of or is based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of material fact, or the omission or alleged omission of a material fact in: (i) any registration statement, including any prospectus or any post-effective amendment thereto; or (ii) any material prepared and/or supplied by Distributor or Issuer for use in conjunction with the offer or sale of Annuity Contracts, or (iii) any state registration or other document filed in any state or jurisdiction in order to qualify any Annuity Contracts under the securities laws of such state or jurisdiction. The terms of this provision shall not be impaired by termination of this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: 22. In connection with the offer, sale or servicing of Annuity Contracts, Broker Dealer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless Distributor and Issuer from any damage or expense, including reasonable attorneys' fees, as a result of (a) the negligence, misconduct or wrongful act of Broker Dealer or producers; (b) any violation of any securities or insurance laws, regulations or orders or (c) any actual obligation of the Affiliated Agency under terms of the agreement between Broker Dealer and the Affiliated Agency, including claims by one or more of Registered Representatives for compensation due or to become due on account of such Registered Representatives' sale or servicing of Annuity Contracts and any claims or controversy between Broker Dealer and Affiliated Agency as to rights to compensation. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Broker Dealer shall not indemnify and hold harmless Distributor and Issuer from any damage or expense on account of the negligence, misconduct or wrongful act of Broker Dealer or producer if such negligence, misconduct or wrongful act arises out of or is based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of material fact, or the omission or alleged omission of a material fact in: (i) any registration statement, including any prospectus or any post-effective amendment thereto; or (ii) any material prepared and/or supplied by Distributor or Issuer for use in conjunction with the offer or sale of Annuity Contracts, or (iii) any state registration or other document filed in any state or jurisdiction in order to qualify any Annuity Contracts under the securities laws of such state or jurisdiction. The terms of this provision shall be impaired by termination of this Agreement, but only for claims arising six months after the termination date.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the survivability of the indemnification clause after termination. Originally, it stated that "The terms of this provision shall not be impaired by termination of this Agreement.". Now, the changed text stipulates that "The terms of this provision shall be impaired by termination of this Agreement, but only for claims arising six months after the termination date." This creates ambiguity, as the initial statement suggest the terms survive termination in any and all cases, while the changed text suggests the Indemnification clause doesn't survive termination if a claim is raised six months after termination. This in-text contradiction can lead to legal disputes, as it is not clear on what basis indemnification would be required after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 22. In connection with the offer, sale or servicing of Annuity Contracts, Broker Dealer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless Distributor and Issuer from any damage or expense, including reasonable attorneys' fees, as a result of (a) the negligence, misconduct or wrongful act of Broker Dealer or producers; (b) any violation of any securities or insurance laws, regulations or orders or (c) any actual obligation of the Affiliated Agency under terms of the agreement between Broker Dealer and the Affiliated Agency, including claims by one or more of Registered Representatives for compensation due or to become due on account of such Registered Representatives' sale or servicing of Annuity Contracts and any claims or controversy between Broker Dealer and Affiliated Agency as to rights to compensation. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Broker Dealer shall not indemnify and hold harmless Distributor and Issuer from any damage or expense on account of the negligence, misconduct or wrongful act of Broker Dealer or producer if such negligence, misconduct or wrongful act arises out of or is based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of material fact, or the omission or alleged omission of a material fact in: (i) any registration statement, including any prospectus or any post-effective amendment thereto; or (ii) any material prepared and/or supplied by Distributor or Issuer for use in conjunction with the offer or sale of Annuity Contracts, or (iii) any state registration or other document filed in any state or jurisdiction in order to qualify any Annuity Contracts under the securities laws of such state or jurisdiction. The terms of this provision shall be impaired by termination of this Agreement, but only for claims arising six months after the termination date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which are identical in this instance. The original clause stated that the indemnification provision would not be impaired by termination of the agreement. The changed text states that the provision 'shall be impaired by termination of this Agreement, but only for claims arising six months after the termination date.' This creates a direct contradiction regarding the survivability of the indemnification clause after termination, thus resulting in an in-text contradiction.
